volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Rope (Section 3.4.720 - Section 3.5.786)

sections.label: Section 3.4.720
sections.translation: They are .

sections.label: Section 3.4.720b
sections.original: Agedum ergo , tange utramvis digitulo minimo modo .
sections.translation: Just come then , touch either of them with your little finger only .

sections.label: Section 3.4.721
sections.translation: What if I do touch them ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.721b
sections.original: Extemplo hercle ego te follem pugilatorium
sections.translation: That very instant , upon my faith , I ’ ll make a hand-ball [*] of you , and while you ’ re in the air I ’ ll belabour you with my fists , you most perjured villain .

sections.label: Section 3.4.723
sections.original: Mihi non liceat meas ancillas Veneris de ara abducere ?
sections.translation: Am I not to be allowed to take away my female slaves from the altar of Venus ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.724
sections.original: Non licet : est lex apud nos —
sections.translation: You may not ; such is the law with us .

sections.label: Section 3.4.724b
sections.translation: I ’ ve no concern with your laws ; for my part , I shall at once carry them both away from here [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.4.726
sections.original: tu , senex , si istas amas , huc arido argentost opus ;
sections.translation: If you are in love with them , old gentleman ( holding out his hand ) , you must down here with the ready cash .

sections.label: Section 3.4.727
sections.original: si autem Veneri complacuerunt , habeat , si argentum dabit .
sections.translation: But these women have proved pleasing to Venus .

sections.label: Section 3.4.728
sections.original: Det tibi argentum ? nunc adeo meam ut scias sententiam ,
sections.translation: A Goddess , pay you money ? Now then , that you may understand my determination , only do you commence in mere joke to offer them the very slightest violence ;

sections.label: Section 3.4.730
sections.original: ita ego te hinc ornatum amittam , tu ipsus te ut non noveris .
sections.translation: I ’ ll send you away from here with such a dressing , that you won ’ t know your own self . You , therefore ( turning to his SERVANTS ) , when I give you the signal , if you don ’ t beat his eyes out of his head , I ’ ll trim you round about with rods just like beds of myrtle [*] with bulrushes .

sections.label: Section 3.4.733
sections.translation: You are treating me with violence .

sections.label: Section 3.4.733b
sections.translation: What , do you even upbraid us with violence , you flagrant specimen of flagitiousness ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.734
sections.original: Tun , trifurcifer , mihi audes inclementer dicere ?
sections.translation: You , you thrice-dotted villain [*] , do you dare to speak abusively to me ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.735
sections.original: Fateor , ego trifurcifer sum , tu es homo adprime probus :
sections.translation: I am a thrice-dotted villain ; I confess it ; you are a strictly honorable man ; ought these women a bit the less to be free ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.736b
sections.translation: What — free ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.737
sections.original: Atque eras tuas quidem hercle , atque ex germana Graecia ;
sections.translation: Aye , and your mistresses , too , I ’ faith , and from genuine Greece [*] ; for one of them was born at Athens of free-born parents .

sections.label: Section 3.4.739
sections.translation: What is it I hear from you ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.739b
sections.translation: That she ( pointing to PALAESTRA ) was born at Athens , a free-born woman .

sections.label: Section 3.4.740
sections.translation: Prithee is she a countrywoman of mine ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.740b
sections.translation: Are you not a Cyrenian ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.741
sections.original: Immo Athenis natus altusque educatusque Atticis .
sections.translation: No ; born at Athens in Attica , bred and educated there .

sections.label: Section 3.4.742
sections.translation: Prithee , aged sir , do protect your countrywomen .

sections.label: Section 3.4.742b
sections.translation: O daughter , when I look on her , separated from me you remind me of my miseries : ( aloud ) she who was lost by me when three years old ; now , if she is living , she ’ s just about as tall , I ’ m sure , as she .

sections.label: Section 3.4.745
sections.original: Argentum ego pro istisce ambabus cuiae erant domino dedi ;
sections.translation: I paid the money down for these two , to their owners , of whatever country they were . What matters it to me whether they were born at Athens or at Thebes , so long as they are rightfully in servitude as my slaves ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.747b
sections.translation: Is it so , you impudent fellow ? What , are you , a cat prowling after maidens , to be keeping children here kidnapped from their parents and destroying them in your disgraceful calling ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.750
sections.original: nam húic alterae quae patria sit profecto nescio ,
sections.translation: But as for this other one , I really don ’ t know what her country is ; I only know that she ’ s more deserving than yourself , you most abominable rascal .

sections.label: Section 3.4.752
sections.translation: Are these women your property ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.752b
sections.original: Contende ergo , uter sit tergo — verior :
sections.translation: Come to the trial , then , which of the two according to his back is the more truthful ; if you don ’ t bear more compliments [*] upon your back than any ship of war [*] has nails , then I ’ m the greatest of liars .

sections.label: Section 3.4.755
sections.original: postea aspicito meum , quando ego tuom inspectavero :
sections.translation: Afterwards , do you examine mine , when I ’ ve examined yours ; if it shall not prove to be so untouched , that any leather flask maker [*] will say that it is a hide most capital and most sound for the purposes of his business , what reason is there why I shouldn ’ t mangle you with stripes , even till you have your belly full ? Why do you stare at them ? If you touch them I ’ ll tear your eyes out .

sections.label: Section 3.4.760
sections.original: At qui , quia votas , utramque iam mecum abducam simul .
sections.translation: Yet notwithstanding , although you forbid me to do so , I ’ ll at once carry them off both together with me .

sections.label: Section 3.4.761
sections.translation: What will you do ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.761b
sections.translation: I ’ ll bring Vulcan ; he is an enemy to Venus [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.4.762
sections.translation: Whither is he going ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.762b
sections.translation: Hallo ! Is there anybody here ? Hallo ! I say .

sections.label: Section 3.4.762c
sections.translation: If you touch the door , that very instant , upon my faith , you shall get a harvest upon your face with fists for your pitchforks [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.4.764
sections.original: Nullum habemus ignem , ficis victitamus aridis .
sections.translation: We keep no fire , we live upon dried figs .

sections.label: Section 3.4.765
sections.original: Ego dabo ignem , siquidem in capite tuo conflandi copiast .
sections.translation: I ’ ll find the fire , if only I have the opportunity of kindling it upon your head .

sections.label: Section 3.4.766
sections.translation: Faith , I ’ ll go somewhere to look for some fire .

sections.label: Section 3.4.766b
sections.translation: What , when you ’ ve found it ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.767
sections.translation: I ’ ll be making a great fire here .

sections.label: Section 3.4.767b
sections.translation: What , to be burning [*] a mortuary sacrifice for yourself ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.768
sections.original: Immo hasce ambas hic in ara ut vivas comburam , id volo .
sections.translation: No , but I ’ ll burn both of these alive here upon the altar .

sections.label: Section 3.4.769
sections.original: Iam hercle ego te barba continuo arripiam , in ignem coniciam
sections.translation: I ’ d like that . For , by my troth , I ’ ll forthwith seize you by the head and throw you into the fire ,

sections.label: Section 3.4.770
sections.original: teque ambustulatum obiciam magnis avibus pabulum .
sections.translation: and , half-roasted , I ’ ll throw you out as food for the great birds . ( Aside .) When I come to a consideration of it with myself , this is that ape , that wanted to take away those swallows from the nest against my will , as I was dreaming in my sleep .

sections.label: Section 3.4.773b
sections.translation: Aged sir , do you know what I request of you ? That you will protect these females and defend them from violence , until I fetch my master .

sections.label: Section 3.4.775
sections.translation: Go look for your master , and fetch him here .

sections.label: Section 3.4.775b
sections.translation: But don ’ t let him —

sections.label: Section 3.4.775c
sections.translation: At his own extreme peril , if he touches them , or if he attempts to do so .

sections.label: Section 3.4.776b
sections.translation: Take care .

sections.label: Section 3.4.776c
sections.translation: Due care is taken ; do you be off .

sections.label: Section 3.4.777
sections.original: Hunc quoque adserva ipsum , ne quo abitat ; nam promisimus
sections.translation: And watch him too , that he doesn ’ t go away anywhere . For we have promised either to give the executioner a great talent , or else to produce this fellow this very day .

sections.label: Section 3.4.779
sections.original: Abi modo , ego dum hoc curabo recte .
sections.translation: Do you only be off . I ’ ll not let him get away , while you are absent .

sections.label: Section 3.4.779b
sections.translation: I ’ ll be back here soon .

sections.label: Section 3.5.780
sections.original: Vtrum tu , leno , cum malo lubentius
sections.translation: Which , you Procurer , had you rather do , be quiet with a thrashing , or e ’ en as it is , without the thrashing , if you had the choice ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.782
sections.original: Ego quae tu loquere flocci non facio , senex .
sections.translation: Old fellow , I don ’ t care a straw for what you say . My own women , in fact , I shall drag away this instant from the altar by the hair , in spite of yourself , and Venus , and supreme Jove .

sections.label: Section 3.5.784b
sections.translation: Just touch them .

sections.label: Section 3.5.785
sections.translation: I ’ troth , I surely will touch them .

sections.label: Section 3.5.785b
sections.translation: Just come then ; only approach this way .

sections.label: Section 3.5.786
sections.original: Iube dúm recedere istos ambo illuc modo .
sections.translation: Only bid both those fellows , then , to move away from there .
